Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Produces 2-AG and related 2-monoacylglycerols from DAGs; major contributor to brain 2-AG pools and synaptic endocannabinoid signaling
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. DGL-alpha is a core diacylglycerol lipase isoform that catalyzes on-demand formation of 2-AG from DAG. 2. It is highly enriched in brain and nervous system, especially postsynaptic dendritic/spine regions. 3. Knockout in mice causes about 80% reduction of brain 2-AG, impairs adult neurogenesis, and abolishes multiple forms of endocannabinoid-mediated synaptic plasticity.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Abundant in brain and nervous system; also expressed in adipose tissue and liver
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Postsynaptic sites in spinal cord, cerebellum, ventral tegmental area, striatum, basolateral amygdala, hippocampus, prefrontal cortex; dendritic surface and spine neck base of Purkinje cells; hippocampal pyramidal cell spines
Common_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Postsynaptic somatodendritic membrane; dendritic surface; spine neck base
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Diacylglycerol lipase; lipase_3 domain; α/β-hydrolase superfamily abH23; N-terminal 4TM region in animal DGLs
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Knockout mice; tissue localization; metabolite profiling of 2-AG; genetic inactivation studies on synaptic plasticity
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: This is a review; evidence summarized from mammalian studies, especially mouse and human. Vertebrate DGL-alpha is 1042 aa and distinct from DGL-beta though functionally convergent. DGL-alpha is indispensable for retrograde synaptic signaling, whereas DGL-beta is not.
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Comparative phylogenetic/genome database mining; no primary omics accession central to review extracted
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Endocannabinoid signaling; synaptic plasticity; axonal growth and guidance; neurogenesis; neurotransmission modulation
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Genetic inactivation impairs adult neurogenesis and synaptic plasticity
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential therapeutic target in nervous system and gastrointestinal disorders; inhibition may modulate inflammatory and neurodegenerative processes
Common_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: CB receptors pathway components; PLC-beta (pathway upstream)

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Produces 2-AG and related 2-monoacylglycerols from DAGs; contributes relatively more to immune/digestive and liver-associated 2-AG regulation than DGL-alpha
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. DGL-beta is the second major mammalian sn-1 diacylglycerol lipase isoform generating 2-AG from DAG. 2. It is relatively abundant in immune system and digestive tract and is implicated in pro-inflammatory responses in macrophages. 3. DGL-beta knockout has limited effect on brain synaptic endocannabinoid signaling compared with DGL-alpha, but DGL-beta is more important for regulating liver 2-AG.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Relatively abundant in immune system and digestive tract; transcripts detectable in adult hippocampus
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Immune system, digestive tract, macrophages, adult hippocampus, liver
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Diacylglycerol lipase; lipase_3 domain; α/β-hydrolase superfamily abH23; animal DGL family
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Knockout mice; transcript detection; pharmacological inhibition; metabolite profiling
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: This is a review. Vertebrate DGL-beta is 543 aa. DGL-beta is evolutionarily older than DGL-alpha according to review interpretation. Brain synaptic eCB signaling is not markedly impaired by DGL-beta genetic inactivation.
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Comparative phylogenetic/genome database mining; no primary omics accession central to review extracted
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Endocannabinoid signaling; inflammatory regulation; gut physiology
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Immune cells/macrophages; digestive tract; liver; hippocampus
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Inhibition suppresses pro-inflammatory mediators, which may alter immune responses
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential therapeutic target for inflammatory disorders and gastrointestinal disease; DGL-beta-selective inhibitors may reduce AA, prostaglandins and TNF-alpha in macrophages
Common_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: CB receptors pathway components; inflammatory mediator pathways

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Encodes DGL isoforms required for phototransduction and associated with lifespan/oxidative stress phenotypes through DAG metabolism
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. inaE encodes two DGL isoforms, INAE-A and INAE-D, generated by alternative splicing. 2. Both proteins hydrolyze DAG with strong sn-1 preference, and INAE-D has about 20-fold higher lipase activity than INAE-A. 3. inaE function is required to couple rhodopsin photoexcitation to TRP/TRPL channel opening, and increased DGL/inaE expression extends lifespan and oxidative stress resistance.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly expressed in eye; also throughout the head
Common_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Photoreceptor cytoplasm; some puncta in rhabdomeres
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Alternative splice isoform characterization; lipase activity assays; antibody localization; mutant/overexpression phenotyping
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: In insects, AA is scarce and canonical CB receptors are absent, so DGL-produced MAG functions may differ from vertebrates. Review summarizes effects on phototransduction and longevity.
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Phototransduction signaling; possible stress response modulation
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: inaE mutants show shortened lifespan and reduced oxidative stress tolerance
Common_Genes.Source_Species_Variety: Oregon-RS mentioned for metabolite measurements, but gene source variety not defined
Common_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: Oregon-RS; Canton-S; w1118 mentioned for metabolite surveys
Common_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: TRP; TRPL; rhodopsin pathway components; TOR/S6K signaling components

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: DGL enzyme linked to longevity and oxidative stress resistance; reduced function shortens lifespan
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. F42G9.6/dagl-1 is described as a DGL enzyme in C. elegans. 2. Inhibition of its post-transcription reduces longevity in response to E. coli noncoding RNAs. 3. Overexpression of dagl-1 extends lifespan and oxidative stress responses, whereas mutants shorten lifespan and elevate p-S6K, implicating TOR/S6K signaling.
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Oxidative stress; bacterial ncRNA DsrA exposure
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Post-transcriptional inhibition; overexpression; mutant phenotyping; signaling readout of p-S6K
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Review-level summary; specific metabolite output not quantified for this gene in the excerpt, but DGL function is tied to DAG metabolism and 2-AG system in nematodes.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Reduced function shortens lifespan and lowers oxidative stress tolerance

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydrolyzes diacylglycerol to monoacylglycerol and free fatty acid; can also synthesize MAG from oleic acid and glycerol
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: DAG; oleic acid and glycerol in esterification/synthesis context
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Produces MAG as main product with no TAG production and displays DAG/MAG selectivity useful for industry
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PCL was the first characterized fungal DGL and is highly selective for MAG/DAG rather than TAG. 2. It synthesizes MAG as a main product from oleic acid and glycerol without positional specificity. 3. Its substrate selectivity makes it useful for industrial lipid modification and epoxidized oil applications.
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: lipase_3 domain; α/β-hydrolase superfamily abH23; N-terminal signal peptide
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Biochemical enzyme characterization; structural modeling/homology analysis; industrial biocatalysis assays
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The region near H259 contributes to unique substrate specificity. PCL 3D structure has been elucidated. Also catalyzes epoxidation reactions.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Industrial production of MAG and modified oils
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Cold/mesophilic optimum may limit broader industrial application
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful biocatalyst for MAG production, oil/fat modification, and epoxidized vegetable oil applications

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydrolyzes DAG to MAG and free fatty acid with strict selectivity against TAG; structure explains exclusion of third acyl chain
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Strict DAG/MAG-selective lipase with defined structural determinants of substrate selectivity and industrial value
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SMG1 is one of the best-characterized microbial DGLs with solved crystal structure. 2. The catalytic triad is Ser171-Asp228-His281, and residues including L103, F278, N102 and Q282 shape lid gating and substrate selectivity. 3. Mutations can broaden TAG acceptance or alter activity/thermostability, highlighting engineering potential.
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: lipase_3 domain; α/β-hydrolase core; catalytic triad Ser171/Asp228/His281; lid loop; abH23 superfamily
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Crystal structure (PDB 3UUE); mutagenesis; in silico analysis; enzyme assays
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: SMG1 is considered potential anti-dandruff target. F278 and N102 form gating mechanism; Q282L enables TAG utilization; N277D improves thermostability; full activation requires lid mobility.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Low optimal temperature may hinder industrial deployment
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong enzyme-engineering target for improved thermostability, altered substrate spectrum, anti-dandruff inhibitor design, and oil/fat modification

Pathway_Genes.Enzyme_Name: Malassezia globosa putative diacylglycerol lipase-like enzyme
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: monoacylglycerol/free fatty acid products from DAG or ester substrates
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: DGL-like enzyme preferring medium chain-length esters, with highest activity toward p-nitrophenyl caprylate
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: supports precursor/product turnover rather than specific terminal nutrient accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Cold-active DGL-like enzyme with medium-chain substrate preference
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MgMDL2 is a putative DGL-like enzyme from M. globosa. 2. It prefers medium-chain esters and shows highest activity toward p-nitrophenyl caprylate. 3. It is cold-active with an optimum at 15 °C and remains active at low temperature.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Recombinant enzyme assay; temperature stability characterization
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The review calls it a putative DGL-like enzyme rather than a fully established canonical DGL.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful source for cold-active industrial lipase engineering

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydrolyzes DAG/MAG-selective substrates; substrate selectivity derives from a bridge-like binding pocket formed by bulky residues
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Microbial DGL with unique bridge-like substrate-binding pocket conferring size-selective DAG preference
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AOL is a fungal DGL with optimum temperature around 30 °C. 2. Its substrate selectivity is attributed to a bridge-like structure formed by W89 and F257 over the catalytic site. 3. A conserved F-X-X-H motif participates in substrate recognition of microbial DGLs.
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: lipase_3 domain; F-X-X-H motif; α/β-hydrolase lipase
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The bridge-like pocket prevents large substrates from entering active site.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate for engineering substrate specificity in industrial lipid synthesis

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Fungal DGL hydrolyzing DAG to MAG/free fatty acid
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Annotated fungal DGL with mesophilic activity profile
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PcMDL is listed as a fungal DGL in the review. 2. Its optimal temperature is about 35 °C. 3. It contributes to the diversity of known microbial DGL biocatalysts.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Limited mechanistic detail provided in excerpt.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Mesophilic optimum may constrain process conditions
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Additional microbial DGL resource for industrial biocatalysis
